name: Notion
one_liner: An AI-powered all-in-one workspace that combines docs, wikis, databases, project management, calendar, and email into a single collaborative platform for individuals and enterprises.

ambiguities: Headcount varies widely across sources: Unify reports ~1,217, press/taptwice report ~800, PitchBook ~4,800, Tracxn ~5,875, Revelio ~4,296, LeadIQ ~4,700 — the ~1,217 from Unify GTM (most granular third-party tracker) is used as the best estimate for full-time direct employees; the 800 figure from press likely reflects an older or narrower count.

Total primary funding raised is ~$343M across 3 primary rounds (Seed ~$2M, Series A $10M, Series B $50M, Series C $275M); the January 2026 $270M tender was a secondary transaction (shares sold by employees, not primary capital raised by the company) and is therefore not included in total_raised_usd. Tracxn cites $418M across 9 rounds, which may include smaller undisclosed rounds.
The last_round field represents the January 2026 secondary tender offer at $11B valuation; it is not a primary equity raise.
Tech stack is inferred from official release notes, OpenAI case study, and VentureBeat reporting; no official stack disclosure found.
ARR figures range from $500M (Sacra, Sept 2025) to $600M (Latka/Forbes, late 2025/early 2026); $600M used as latest estimate.
